title: Apartments for Rent Near Cleveland State University
metaTitle: Apartments Near Cleveland State University, OH
metaDescription: Searching for apartments near Cleveland State University downtown? See rent ranges, transit along Euclid Avenue, and how to book a showing nearby.
answerBox: Cleveland State University sits in Cleveland's Downtown statistical planning area, along the Euclid Avenue corridor near Public Square and the Gateway district. Rent Finder Cleveland's closest managed rental homes sit just southeast in Central and Fairfax (ZIP 44104), a short distance from campus along Euclid or Chester Avenue.

sections.h2: Renting an apartment near Cleveland State University
body: Cleveland State University sits within Cleveland's Downtown statistical planning area, the city's central business district anchored by Public Square and the Terminal Tower/Tower City rail hub, with the Gateway sports district and Warehouse District nearby. Housing directly around campus leans toward larger apartment buildings and student-oriented complexes rather than single-family rental homes.

Rent Finder Cleveland doesn't currently manage units inside Downtown itself. Our closest inventory sits a short distance southeast, in the **Central and Fairfax** neighborhoods (**ZIP 44104**), reachable from campus along the Euclid Avenue or Chester Avenue corridors. [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) and tell our team what you're looking for, and we'll point you toward the closest voucher-friendly home we manage.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent near Cleveland State?
body: There's no campus-specific rent index, so citywide figures are the closest available benchmark. Per **Zumper's** rent report dated July 4, 2026, Cleveland's citywide 1-bedroom median is **$1,195/month** and 2-bedroom is **$1,100**. Per **RentCafe's (Yardi)** report dated July 2, 2026, the average across professionally-managed apartment buildings — the type most common right around downtown — is **$1,564/month** for 787 square feet, with 1-bedrooms averaging **$1,451**.

Homes in our own portfolio in Central and Fairfax, the closest neighborhoods to campus where we manage rental homes, generally fall within our citywide range of about **$700 to $1,800 a month**, with most closer to **$1,000**.

sections.h2: Getting to and from Cleveland State without a car
body: Cleveland State sits directly on the HealthLine, GCRTA's bus-rapid-transit route running the length of Euclid Avenue between Public Square and University Circle, with continued service to East Cleveland. Tower City, the hub where the Red, Blue, and Green rail lines converge, is a short walk or ride away, giving direct connections toward Cleveland Hopkins Airport, Shaker Heights, and the east side.

items: **Location:** Downtown Cleveland, Euclid Avenue corridor near Public Square and Gateway
**Transit:** HealthLine (Euclid Avenue BRT); Tower City rail hub (Red/Blue/Green lines) nearby
**Rent Finder Cleveland's nearest inventory:** Central and Fairfax neighborhoods (ZIP 44104)
**Distance to nearest inventory:** A short ride southeast along Euclid or Chester Avenue

sections.h2: Section 8 and vouchers near Cleveland State
body: We don't currently manage units directly downtown, but every one of the 90+ rental homes we manage across Greater Cleveland — including our nearest homes in Central and Fairfax — accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. Ohio has no statewide law requiring a landlord to accept a voucher, and Cleveland does not currently have a citywide source-of-income protection ordinance, so acceptance at any specific downtown building should be confirmed with that building's owner.

See our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how CMHA's voucher process works, or the full [apartments for rent in Cleveland, OH](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page for everything we currently manage across the city.

Students and staff who don't need to be immediately adjacent to campus sometimes look east toward Central and Fairfax, or further into University Circle, for a wider range of housing types and price points. Fairfax sits directly adjacent to the Cleveland Clinic's main campus, and Central is the historic district just southeast of Downtown — both are a short ride from Cleveland State along Euclid or Chester Avenue rather than a walk.

For a broader comparison of housing near Cleveland's other major institutions, see our [apartments near University Circle](/cleveland-rentals/apartments/university-circle-apartments-for-rent-cleveland/) and [apartments near Cleveland Clinic](/cleveland-rentals/apartments/apartments-near-cleveland-clinic/) guides, or the citywide [houses for rent in Cleveland, OH](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page if a full house rather than an apartment fits your search better.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage apartments near Cleveland State University?
faq.a: Not directly downtown — our portfolio doesn't currently include units inside the Downtown statistical planning area. Our nearest managed homes sit in the Central and Fairfax neighborhoods (ZIP 44104), a short ride from campus. Book a free showing and tell our team what you need.

faq.q: How much does an apartment cost near Cleveland State University?
faq.a: There's no campus-specific index, but citywide, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report shows a $1,195 median for 1-bedrooms, while RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report shows a $1,451 average for professionally-managed buildings, the type more common downtown. Our own portfolio nearby in Central/Fairfax runs about $700 to $1,800.

faq.q: Where exactly is Cleveland State University located?
faq.a: Cleveland State University sits within Cleveland's Downtown statistical planning area, along the Euclid Avenue corridor near Public Square, Tower City, and the Gateway sports district.

faq.q: How do I get to Cleveland State without a car?
faq.a: The HealthLine bus-rapid-transit route runs directly on Euclid Avenue past campus, connecting Public Square to University Circle and East Cleveland. Tower City, where the Red, Blue, and Green RTA rail lines converge, is also a short walk away.

faq.q: Are Section 8 apartments available near Cleveland State?
faq.a: We don't manage units downtown currently, but every home in our broader portfolio, including our nearest homes in Central and Fairfax, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Acceptance at any specific downtown building varies by owner, since Ohio has no statewide law requiring it.
